commands.review_summary:
The PR adds ChatGPT browser file artifact discovery, cookie-backed downloading, session artifact metadata, archive guarding, tests, and docs.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows the PR accepts any ChatGPT URL containing `/backend-api/` and later fetches it with the browser cookie header. I did not run a live ChatGPT artifact smoke.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the PR lacks real browser proof and the remaining blocker is a security boundary decision around authenticated ChatGPT backend URL scope.

target.title: [codex] save ChatGPT browser file artifacts
target.body:
## Summary

Implements the narrow file-artifact boundary proposed in steipete/oracle#244:

- add `kind: "file"` session artifacts for ChatGPT browser runs
- discover downloadable file anchors from the assistant response only
- download only ChatGPT-owned `/backend-api/` file URLs with the existing browser cookies
- treat `sandbox:/mnt/data/...` as source metadata / filename hints, not arbitrary fetch targets
- include saved files in the existing transcript artifact list and archive guard

This intentionally does not fetch arbitrary external links from the answer.

## Why

After #169, browser sessions persist transcripts, Deep Research reports, and generated images, but generic ChatGPT-generated files such as ZIPs, wheels, source distributions, CSVs, or PDFs were still stranded in the live ChatGPT conversation. This extends the same artifact layer without adding a new public API.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec vitest run tests/browser/artifacts.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptFiles.test.ts tests/browser/chatgptImages.test.ts tests/browser/sessionRunner.test.ts tests/cli/sessionRunner.test.ts`
- `pnpm run check`

## Notes

- No live browser smoke was run in this branch.
- The archive guard now requires detected browser file artifacts to be saved before auto-archiving, matching the existing generated-image safety model.
